step_by_step_reasoning: The user-facing interfaces (Web, Mobile/SMS, Alexa) on the left side initiate requests to the central Amazon Lex bot. Lex coordinates with DynamoDB for building hours, queries partner databases for external menus/info, analyzes queries with Comprehend and Elasticsearch, and notifies content owners of negative feedback via SNS.

graph.name: St. Louis University Q&A Chatbot
graph.notes: Multi-channel Q&A chatbot using Amazon Lex to answer student questions, integrated with DynamoDB, OpenSearch, Comprehend, and partner databases.

nodes.name: Web Browser
nodes.notes: Students and faculty asking questions via the university website.

nodes.name: Mobile Device
nodes.notes: Students querying the bot via SMS text messages.

nodes.name: Alexa Speaker
nodes.notes: Alexa devices in student residence halls.

nodes.name: Amazon Lex
nodes.notes: Core bot engine that parses user intent and routes requests.

nodes.name: Amazon DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Stores default, holiday, and break hours for university buildings.

nodes.name: Food Vendor Database
nodes.notes: External database containing daily dining schedules and menus.

nodes.name: Partner Database
nodes.notes: Second external partner database integrated with the chatbot.

nodes.name: Amazon SNS
nodes.notes: Sends real-time email alerts to content owners upon negative user feedback.

nodes.name: Amazon Comprehend
nodes.notes: Hones in on keywords and subjects while filtering out common linking words.

nodes.name: Elasticsearch
nodes.notes: Provides real-time scoring to evaluate how questions map to configured answers.

edges.notes: Web query sent to Lex bot.
SMS query sent to Lex bot.
Alexa voice query sent to Lex bot.
Lex retrieves building hours from DynamoDB.
Lex fetches menu data from external partner database.
Lex evaluates question mapping and scoring in Elasticsearch.
Lex uses Comprehend to analyze user sentiment and extract keywords.
Lex triggers SNS notification for negative feedback.
